title: cordia macrophylla
intro: Cordia macrophylla, also known as the Wild Peach or Bael, is a species of flowering plant in the bignonia family, native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Cordia macrophylla include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support digestive health, among others. Cordia macrophylla can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used to treat a range of ailments in traditional medicine.
